title: named hooks: declaration/required/call-position
manager: Named hooks as a machine's dependency contract — callbacks the machine declares (`hook f(a:T)->R [required]`), that the caller must supply, typed like everything else and called at explicit positions in the logic. This is how a machine states "I need you to provide this behavior," turning host integration from ad-hoc wiring into a checked interface, and return values are recorded so replay never re-fires the outside world.
cs: `hook f(a:T)->R [required];` declaration; call-position `call f(x)` / `assign y := call f(x)`; effect boundary per §11; the return-recording format lands v13 (slot reserved) — v11 ships call semantics + live mode.
